🇮🇷 🤡 🇺🇸 ✈️ Urgent / Military source to Tasnim: US seeks to bomb and kill its own pilot in Iran ➡️ A military source told Tasnim News Agency: After giving up hope of rescuing the fighter pilot who was shot down by Iranian forces two days ago, the Americans are now trying to bomb and kill him in locations where they think he might be hiding. ➡️ The source noted that several US fighter jets bombed areas in Kohgiluyeh County tonight because they believed their pilot was there. ➡️ The source said the Americans have lost hope of finding the second crew member, so they now want to kill him by bombing multiple sites. ➡️ When asked whether this individual is currently in Iranian custody, the source declined to comment, saying: "We're not saying anything about that yet. But what we can tell you is that even about the first pilot — the one they claim to have rescued — the Americans aren't telling the whole truth." ➡️ According to the source, the story of these downed fighter pilots will turn into yet another cover-up scandal for the Americans. 🐻 If Iranians were smart (which of course, they're not and they can't be - right, Ameribros?) they would collect the navigator who ejected - and use his personal locator to lure in the US CSAR team... And make the US go on a wild goose chase, lasting for hours - while normal CSAR operations are fast in and out extractions... Who knows? Who knows? And yes - the A-10's and F-35's accompanying the CSAR team are there to democratically and totally not warcrime-ishly "shock and awe" the "enemies" - military and police forces and local population on the ground... And just might get themselves shot at or even down as well... But only if Iranians are the good guys in this H-wood movie. They can't be, right? Cuz then it's not a real Hollywood movie, right? 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

U.S. intelligence says Iran is using decoys, making it hard to tell which targets are real. This creates uncertainty about how many actual missile systems have been destroyed, since some strikes may have hit fakes instead of real launchers. Source: NYT

Top advisers around Trump have been privately arguing that Iran’s infrastructure—like power plants and road networks—can be treated as legitimate military targets. Pete Hegseth and others have told Trump that striking these systems could disrupt Iran’s missile and drone capabilities, and even create internal instability that slows its nuclear progress. Source: WSJ
